{
  "judge_id": "your_judge_id",
  "active_set_recognized": ["R1.2", "R1.4", "R1.7", "RX.1", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"],
  "scores": {"R1.2": 3, "R1.4": 3, "R1.7": 3, "RX.1": 3, "RX.3": 5, "RX.4": 5, "RX.5": 3},
  "rationale": {"R1.2": "Identifies a logical error (BLEU as calibration evidence) and proposes a fix (using ECE).", "R1.4": "Names prior work (Monte Carlo dropout, ensemble disagreement) and articulates the delta (single-pass token approach).", "R1.7": "Flags missing citations for specific techniques used in the fine-tuning corpus.", "RX.1": "Identifies uncertainty regarding the generalization of calibration to unseen language pairs.", "RX.3": "Output covers exactly the requested dimensions and does not drift.", "RX.4": "No regressions are introduced, and potentially harmful patterns (over-trusting erroneous translations) are addressed.", "RX.5": "Major rewrites are traced, but minor edits are not."},
  "confidence": {"R1.2": 0.8, "R1.4": 0.7, "R1.7": 0.6, "RX.1": 0.9, "RX.3": 0.9, "RX.4": 0.9, "RX.5": 0.7},
  "blockers": []
}

rationale.R1.2: Identifies a logical error (BLEU as calibration evidence) and proposes a fix (using ECE).
rationale.R1.4: Names prior work (Monte Carlo dropout, ensemble disagreement) and articulates the delta (single-pass token approach).
rationale.R1.7: Flags missing citations for specific techniques used in the fine-tuning corpus.
rationale.RX.1: Identifies uncertainty regarding the generalization of calibration to unseen language pairs.
rationale.RX.3: Output covers exactly the requested dimensions and does not drift.
rationale.RX.4: No regressions are introduced, and potentially harmful patterns (over-trusting erroneous translations) are addressed.
rationale.RX.5: Major rewrites are traced, but minor edits are not.
